title: Frozen Desserts
content:
FROZEN DESSERTS

CLASSIFICATION

Water Ices: Sugar, water, fruit juices. Often served with meat course.

Frappes: Water ice frozen to mushy consistency. Often served as punch.

Sherbets: Water ice with milk, cream, stiffly beaten egg whites, or gelatin added.

Plain Ice Cream: Thin cream, sugar, crushed sweetened fruit or flavoring.

Custard Ice Cream: Soft custard, thin cream, flavoring.

Sundaes: Ice cream with sweet sauce or crushed fruit.

Parfaits: Well-beaten egg yolks or stiffly beaten whites, heavy syrup, stiffly whipped cream. Packed in mold; frozen without agitation.

Mousses: Sweetened, flavored, stiffly whipped cream. Packed in mold; frozen without agitation.

FREEZING EQUIPMENT

Crank Freezers: Wood or metal tub for ice/salt; metal container for mixture. Dasher turned by crank whips mixture as it freezes. Fill ¾ full. Turn slowly at first until stiffening; then rapidly until firm. Slow turning incorporates air; rapid gives maximum volume and velvety texture.

Vacuum Freezers: Metal; double wall with dead air space. Occasional agitation required.

Fireless Cooker as Freezer: Small utensil with tight cover placed in fireless cooker well; packed with ice and salt (8:1). Occasional stirring required.

Mechanical Refrigerator: Trays for freezing; molds for desserts requiring no agitation.

PREPARATION

Scald all equipment; cool thoroughly. For crank freezers: have waxed paper ready for covering mixture after dasher removed; two stoppers (one for side, one for center of cover).

PROPORTIONS OF ICE AND SALT

8 parts finely crushed ice to 1 part rock salt: For crank freezers, vacuum freezers, fireless cookers
Equal proportions: For freezing desserts in molds
4 parts ice to 1 part salt: For packing already-frozen desserts

METHODS

Crank Freezer: Fill ¾ full. Turn slowly until stiffening; then rapidly until firm. Drain brine adequately to prevent seepage into mixture.

Vacuum/Fireless Cooker/Mechanical Refrigerator: Must have sufficiently heavy body to require little/no stirring. If stirring required: uncover, scrape from sides, beat quickly and thoroughly, cover tightly, repack if necessary.

Molds: Very rich desserts requiring no agitation.

Mechanical Refrigerator Trays: Require more body than crank freezer desserts. Stir at frequent intervals to prevent crystal formation (except mousses/parfaits).

RIPENING (PACKING)

Flavor improves if packed and allowed to stand several hours.

Drain brine. Wipe top with clean damp cloth. Remove dasher. Press mixture down firmly. Cover with waxed paper. Place cover firmly. Insert corks in top and side openings. Repack in ice and salt (4:1). Cover top with 3-4 inches ice. Cover with burlap, canvas, or paper. Allow to stand several hours.

TO SERVE

Remove upper portion of ice and salt. Pour cold water over top. Wipe cover with damp cloth. Remove cover and waxed paper carefully to prevent salt water dripping into contents.
